key_learnings: Nutritional requirements per kg body weight are significantly higher in children than adults due to rapid growth and development. Iron, calcium, vitamin D, and omega-3 DHA are the most critical nutrients for cognitive and skeletal development in children. Establishing healthy eating behaviors and food preferences in early childhood has lifelong dietary impact.
research_findings: Exclusive breastfeeding for 6 months provides optimal nutrition and immune protection in infants per WHO guidelines. Iron deficiency in children under 5 is associated with measurable cognitive and motor development delays. Excess sugar intake in childhood accelerates dental caries and establishes neural reward pathways associated with food addiction.
current_focus_areas: Building age-stratified nutritional guidelines (infants, toddlers, school-age, adolescents); developing parent-facing dietary guidance tools; researching the role of early gut microbiome development in long-term health.
